results.title: Effects of a WHO-guided digital health intervention for depression in Syrian refugees in Lebanon: A randomized controlled trial
results.tldr: In this study, a guided, digital intervention was effective in reducing depression in displaced people in Lebanon and should be made available to communities of displaced people that have digital access.

results.title: The effect of digital health interventions for postpartum depression or anxiety: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials
results.venue: American Journal of Obstetrics and Gynecology
results.tldr: DHI modestly, but significantly, reduced scores assessing PPD and PPA symptoms and more research is needed to identify DHIs that effectively prevent or treat PPD and PPA but encourage ongoing engagement throughout the study period.
results.abstract: DHI modestly, but significantly, reduced scores assessing PPD and PPA symptoms and more research is needed to identify DHIs that effectively prevent or treat PPD and PPA but encourage ongoing engagement throughout the study period.

results.title: Digital Health Interventions for Adult Patients With Cancer Evaluated in Randomized Controlled Trials: Scoping Review
results.tldr: Systematic reviews of the identified digital interventions developed and evaluated in RCTs involving adult patients with cancer are strongly recommended to integrate digital health interventions into clinical practice.
results.abstract: Systematic reviews of the identified digital interventions developed and evaluated in RCTs involving adult patients with cancer are strongly recommended to integrate digital health interventions into clinical practice.

results.title: Effectiveness of digital health interventions for chronic conditions management in European primary care settings: Systematic review and meta-analysis
results.tldr: Digital health interventions have not yet demonstrated substantial benefits over traditional care for chronic disease management in European primary care, and the impact remains limited.
results.abstract: Digital health interventions have not yet demonstrated substantial benefits over traditional care for chronic disease management in European primary care, and the impact remains limited.

results.title: Comprehensive Dynamic Trial Designs for Behavioral Prevention Research With Communities: Overcoming Inadequacies of the Randomized Controlled Trial Paradigm
results.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
               <jats:p>In considering how behavioral prevention research could best lead to sustained risk reduction at community or population levels, we focus on the following question: How does the prevention research paradigm help or hinder our ability to develop sustainable community-level change? “The prevention research paradigm” refers to the routine way in which our scientic community identies, tests, and draws conclusions about strategies to reduce HIV

results.title: Identifying and Categorizing Adverse Events in Trials of Digital Mental Health Interventions: Narrative Scoping Review of Trials in the International Standard Randomized Controlled Trial Number Registry
results.tldr: There is significant variation in the reporting of AEs in trials of digital mental health interventions, which may reflect limited reporting processes and difficulty recognizing AEs related to digital mental health interventions.
results.abstract: There is significant variation in the reporting of AEs in trials of digital mental health interventions, which may reflect limited reporting processes and difficulty recognizing AEs related to digital mental health interventions.

results.title: Improving the management of type 2 diabetes in China using a multifaceted digital health intervention in primary health care: the SMARTDiabetes cluster randomised controlled trial
results.venue: The Lancet Regional Health - Western Pacific
results.tldr: A multifaceted digital health intervention improved T2DM risk factor control rates, particularly in rural communities where there may be stronger relationships between patients and doctors and greater family member support.
results.abstract: A multifaceted digital health intervention improved T2DM risk factor control rates, particularly in rural communities where there may be stronger relationships between patients and doctors and greater family member support.

results.title: Technological functionality and system architecture of mobile health interventions for diabetes management: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials
results.tldr: This meta-analysis confirms that mobile health applications with practical technological functionalities and system architectures are beneficial in managing diabetes, and supports using these applications as valuable tools in diabetes care.
results.abstract: This meta-analysis confirms that mobile health applications with practical technological functionalities and system architectures are beneficial in managing diabetes, and supports using these applications as valuable tools in diabetes care.

results.title: An mHealth Intervention (ReZone) to Help Young People Self-Manage Overwhelming Feelings: Cluster-Randomized Controlled Trial
results.tldr: The findings of this study suggest that ReZone was implemented and adopted, and whether ReZone has the scope to impact change or limitations related to the setting, context, and appropriateness of an RCT is unclear.
results.abstract: The findings of this study suggest that ReZone was implemented and adopted, and whether ReZone has the scope to impact change or limitations related to the setting, context, and appropriateness of an RCT is unclear.

results.title: Eat & Tell: A Randomized Trial of Random-Loss Incentive to Increase Dietary Self-Tracking Compliance
results.tldr: random-loss incentive is introduced -- a new financial incentive based on loss aversion and unpredictability principles -- to address the problem of individuals» growing insensitivity to repeated interventions over time.
results.abstract: random-loss incentive is introduced -- a new financial incentive based on loss aversion and unpredictability principles -- to address the problem of individuals» growing insensitivity to repeated interventions over time.

results.title: Efficacy of polygenic risk scores and digital technologies for INNOvative personalized cardiovascular disease PREVention in high-risk adults: protocol of a randomized controlled trial
results.tldr: The INNOPREV trial will contribute to the advancement of personalized cardiovascular disease prevention, with the potential to positively impact public health and reduce the burden of CVDs on healthcare systems.
results.abstract: The INNOPREV trial will contribute to the advancement of personalized cardiovascular disease prevention, with the potential to positively impact public health and reduce the burden of CVDs on healthcare systems.

results.title: It Is Time to Reconsider Factorial Designs: How Bradford Hill and R. A. Fisher Shaped the Standard of Clinical Evidence
results.tldr: Quality managers, health system leaders, and directors of research institutes could increase productivity and gain important insights by promoting a broader use of factorial designs to study multiple interventions simultaneously and to learn from interactions.
results.abstract: Quality managers, health system leaders, and directors of research institutes could increase productivity and gain important insights by promoting a broader use of factorial designs to study multiple interventions simultaneously and to learn from interactions.

results.title: The impact of digital interventions on health insurance coverage for reproductive, maternal, newborn and child health services utilization in Kakamega, Kenya: a cluster randomized controlled trial
results.tldr: Improved NHIF coverage, providing enhanced access to RMNCH services of unlimited duration at both public and private facilities, did not result in an increased uptake of care, in a context where access to basic public RMNCH services was already widespread.
results.abstract: Improved NHIF coverage, providing enhanced access to RMNCH services of unlimited duration at both public and private facilities, did not result in an increased uptake of care, in a context where access to basic public RMNCH services was already widespread.

results.title: Mediated effects of LIFE4YOUth—a mobile health intervention for multiple lifestyle behavior change among high school students in Sweden: findings from a randomized controlled trial
results.tldr: The observed intervention effects of increased physical activity and fruit and vegetable intake could only to a small extent be explained by increased confidence and know-how, and future studies should explore other potential mediators.
results.abstract: The observed intervention effects of increased physical activity and fruit and vegetable intake could only to a small extent be explained by increased confidence and know-how, and future studies should explore other potential mediators.

results.title: Evaluating the effectiveness and feasibility of a digital health intervention to community-based rehabilitation in older adults: A cluster randomized controlled trial study protocol
results.tldr: This study aimed to develop a community-based rehabilitation management platform for older adults centered around digital health technology, with the plan to conduct a cluster randomized controlled trial to gather more evidence to explore the best practices and service models of community-based rehabilitation based on digital health technology.
results.abstract: This study aimed to develop a community-based rehabilitation management platform for older adults centered around digital health technology, with the plan to conduct a cluster randomized controlled trial to gather more evidence to explore the best practices and service models of community-based rehabilitation based on digital health technology.

Weight Gain Prevention Outcomes From a Pragmatic Digital Health Intervention With Community Health Center Patients: Randomized Controlled Trial
results.tldr: In adults with overweight or obesity receiving primary care at a community health center, the authors did not find long-term evidence to support the dissemination of a digital health intervention for weight gain prevention.

results.title: The effects of shared decision-making informed dietary intervention based on digital health technology in older adults with type 2 diabetes mellitus: A randomized controlled trial
results.tldr: The intervention program based on a digital health system significantly improved glycemic control and enhanced self-care ability in older adults with type 2 diabetes mellitus and can integrate digital health technology in behavior change intervention.
results.abstract: The intervention program based on a digital health system significantly improved glycemic control and enhanced self-care ability in older adults with type 2 diabetes mellitus and can integrate digital health technology in behavior change intervention.

results.title: A randomized controlled trial of a digital lifestyle intervention involving postoperative patients with colorectal cancer
results.tldr: Overall, mobile application use had a minimal effect on postoperative health-related QoL, warranting further research on their efficacy and compliance rates.
results.abstract: Overall, mobile application use had a minimal effect on postoperative health-related QoL, warranting further research on their efficacy and compliance rates.

results.title: Randomized controlled trials of digital health interventions for rheumatic disease management: a systematic review
results.venue: Bulletin of the World Health Organization
results.tldr: Evaluating digital health interventions for rheumatic disease management found many telehealth, wearable technologies and educational interventions significantly improved disease control, patient adherence, knowledge and self-efficacy.
results.abstract: Evaluating digital health interventions for rheumatic disease management found many telehealth, wearable technologies and educational interventions significantly improved disease control, patient adherence, knowledge and self-efficacy.

results.title: Effects of a digital intervention on physical activity in adults: A randomized controlled trial in a large-scale sample
results.tldr: Both programs were effective in promoting PA among adults, with no significant differences observed between the two RCT groups, suggesting that the effectiveness of such programs may not solely depend on their interactivity but also on the quality of the information provided.
results.abstract: Both programs were effective in promoting PA among adults, with no significant differences observed between the two RCT groups, suggesting that the effectiveness of such programs may not solely depend on their interactivity but also on the quality of the information provided.

results.title: A Randomized Controlled Trial of a Digital Intervention to Improve the Sexual Health of Adolescent and Young Adult Male Emergency Department Patients
results.tldr: Dr. Eric is the first intervention to demonstrate evidence of short-term efficacy for improving condom use among male AYA in the emergency department and was feasible to deliver and acceptable to male AYA patients.
results.abstract: Dr. Eric is the first intervention to demonstrate evidence of short-term efficacy for improving condom use among male AYA in the emergency department and was feasible to deliver and acceptable to male AYA patients.

results.title: Effect of Components and Delivery Modes of Digital Health Interventions Targeting Physical Activity in People With a Chronic Condition or Multimorbidity: A Systematic Review and Component Network Meta-Analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials.
results.venue: Journal of Physical Activity and Health
results.tldr: The most effective digital health interventions for promoting physical activity in individuals with chronic conditions include exercise combined with self-management, delivered via mixed technologies, or web/app platforms.
results.abstract: The most effective digital health interventions for promoting physical activity in individuals with chronic conditions include exercise combined with self-management, delivered via mixed technologies, or web/app platforms.

results.title: The impact of digital health interventions on HIV testing uptake among adolescents: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials
results.venue: Journal of Public Health and Development
results.tldr: Digital media intervention significantly increased HIV testing engagement among adolescent and HIV screening programs using digital media are needed to optimize access to HIV testing services.
results.abstract: Digital media intervention significantly increased HIV testing engagement among adolescent and HIV screening programs using digital media are needed to optimize access to HIV testing services.

results.title: Crowdsourcing integrated into a digital mental health platform for anxiety and depression: A pilot randomized controlled trial
results.abstract: A two-armed pilot randomized controlled trial examining two versions of the novel “Overcoming Thoughts” platform, finding neither version of the “Overcoming Thoughts” platform reduced anxiety or depression significantly more than the other.
results.tldr: A two-armed pilot randomized controlled trial examining two versions of the novel “Overcoming Thoughts” platform, finding neither version of the “Overcoming Thoughts” platform reduced anxiety or depression significantly more than the other.

results.title: Effect of a digital health intervention on outpatients with heart failure: a randomized, controlled trial
results.tldr: Although the digital programme did not improve health-related QoL, it led to benefits in other important outcomes such as self-care, disease-specific knowledge, and several key metabolic parameters.
results.abstract: Although the digital programme did not improve health-related QoL, it led to benefits in other important outcomes such as self-care, disease-specific knowledge, and several key metabolic parameters.

results.title: Which transgender and gender diverse groups benefit most from E-health? Subgroup analyses of the randomized controlled trial i2TransHealth in Germany
results.tldr: It was revealed that i2TransHealth demonstrated effectiveness for TGD individuals overall, with higher age and education levels being associated with greater reductions in psychological distress, and e-health interventions like i2TransHealth offer promise in addressing the specific challenges faced by TGD people, particularly those in underserved remote areas.
results.abstract: It was revealed that i2TransHealth demonstrated effectiveness for TGD individuals overall, with higher age and education levels being associated with greater reductions in psychological distress, and e-health interventions like i2TransHealth offer promise in addressing the specific challenges faced by TGD people, particularly those in underserved remote areas.

results.title: Planning, implementation, evaluation, and sustainment of digital health interventions for adolescent substance use prevention: a systematic review of influencing factors based on the RE-AIM framework
results.tldr: A systematic review of school-based digital health interventions in the USA, Europe, and Taiwan found that adoption and implementation factors influencing school participation were less frequently addressed, and maintenance was not mentioned.
results.abstract: A systematic review of school-based digital health interventions in the USA, Europe, and Taiwan found that adoption and implementation factors influencing school participation were less frequently addressed, and maintenance was not mentioned.

results.title: Effectiveness of a guided digital self-help intervention to improve sleep and the biological clock in university students – Study protocol for a randomized controlled trial
results.tldr: This trial builds upon an open feasibility study and will provide evidence of an online guided self-help program for students, and determine the potential wider dissemination of the intervention to address the high need for available and accessible help for students experiencing insomnia.

results.title: Active components in digital health interventions for sleep among adolescents: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials
results.tldr: dCBT-I appears most beneficial for adolescents with insomnia or poor sleep, and future research should refine digital delivery, ensure intervention fidelity, and clarify which active ingredients drive sustained improvements in sleep and mental health.

results.title: Effectiveness of Digital Reproductive Health Education Interventions for Adolescent Girls: A Literature Review
results.tldr: The findings indicate that digital interventions consistently improve adolescent girls’ knowledge and attitudes toward reproductive health and enhance their confidence in accessing health services and suggest that digital interventions should be integrated with supportive approaches, such as family involvement and continuous follow-up, to achieve more effective and sustainable behavioral change.
results.abstract: The findings indicate that digital interventions consistently improve adolescent girls’ knowledge and attitudes toward reproductive health and enhance their confidence in accessing health services and suggest that digital interventions should be integrated with supportive approaches, such as family involvement and continuous follow-up, to achieve more effective and sustainable behavioral change.

results.title: A randomized controlled trial on the application of a chronic disease management platform based on digital health technology combined with an innovative model of intelligent management for hypertension in patients with hypertension
results.tldr: It is expected that a decrease in systolic blood pressure can reduce the risk of cardiovascular events by about 10% and is expected that a decrease of 5 mmHg can reduce the risk of cardiovascular events by about 10%.
results.abstract: It is expected that a decrease in systolic blood pressure can reduce the risk of cardiovascular events by about 10% and is expected that a decrease of 5 mmHg can reduce the risk of cardiovascular events by about 10%.

results.title: Mapping the effectiveness of digital health interventions in promoting electronic cigarette cessation: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials
results.tldr: The study revealed that for any DHI to be successful in its objective, the developers of the interventions must focus on DDOH, as it plays a crucial role in enhancing user experience and adherence to the intended intervention.
results.abstract: The study revealed that for any DHI to be successful in its objective, the developers of the interventions must focus on DDOH, as it plays a crucial role in enhancing user experience and adherence to the intended intervention.

results.title: Aha! moments during brief digital mental health interventions: a mixed methods study of randomized controlled trials
results.tldr: In one trial, but not the other, participants who endorsed Aha! moments showed larger 8-week reductions in loneliness than those who did not, and future work developing SSIs for loneliness may benefit from focusing on cognitive restructuring, exposure, and social skills training.
results.abstract: In one trial, but not the other, participants who endorsed Aha! moments showed larger 8-week reductions in loneliness than those who did not, and future work developing SSIs for loneliness may benefit from focusing on cognitive restructuring, exposure, and social skills training.

results.title: The Effects of Digital Health Interventions for Family Members in Intensive Care Units: Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials
results.tldr: This review emphasizes significant uncertainty in estimated effects and provides the first thorough synthesis of RCT evidence on DHIs for ICU family members, despite its methodological rigor and use of PIs.

results.abstract:
BACKGROUND

One of the main recognizable challenges in the digital mental health interventions field is that users adhere to these interventions in their unguided forms poorly. Studies have shown that a persuasive system design focused on encouraging users to make positive behavior changes in their lives can increase user engagement and a program’s efficacy. results.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
               <jats:p>This chapter explains the circumstances under which the standard randomized controlled trial design might not be a suitable design, or may not be feasible. Common alternative randomized controlled trial designs and trial modifications are considered using example clinical trials. results.venue: EAI Endorsed Transactions on Pervasive Health and Technology
results.tldr: The research results provide empirical evidence that the CMDH-I system's therapeutic effect is achieved through enhanced parasympathetic activity (increased HRV-RMSSD), thus validating its precise neurophysiological mechanism of sleep regulation.
results.abstract: The research results provide empirical evidence that the CMDH-I system's therapeutic effect is achieved through enhanced parasympathetic activity (increased HRV-RMSSD), thus validating its precise neurophysiological mechanism of sleep regulation.

results.venue: International Journal of Technology Assessment in Health Care
results.tldr: This case study shows that real-world data can be used to inform assessments of complex interventions, and traditional evidence-synthesis presentation methods can be adapted to facilitate the inclusion of these data.
results.abstract: This case study shows that real-world data can be used to inform assessments of complex interventions, and traditional evidence-synthesis presentation methods can be adapted to facilitate the inclusion of these data.

results.title: Real-world evidence bridging clinical trials and routine clinical practice to improve patient care
results.tldr: How real-world evidence (RWE) is generated and analyzed using large healthcare databases and advanced analytics is outlined, highlighting the potential role of artificial intelligence in improving data extraction and study reliability while emphasizing the importance of human scientific oversight.
results.abstract: How real-world evidence (RWE) is generated and analyzed using large healthcare databases and advanced analytics is outlined, highlighting the potential role of artificial intelligence in improving data extraction and study reliability while emphasizing the importance of human scientific oversight.

results.title: Self-Reported Health-Related Quality of Life in Patients With Ischemic Heart Disease: A Randomized Controlled Trial on Digital Health Pharmacist Interventions.
results.tldr: There is a significant improvement in HRQoL among patients with ischemia in the intervention group, suggesting that the implementation of DHPIs may an effective strategy for management of IHD.
results.abstract: There is a significant improvement in HRQoL among patients with ischemia in the intervention group, suggesting that the implementation of DHPIs may an effective strategy for management of IHD.

results.title: Effects of a 12-week digital training equipment program on cognitive function and mental health in older women: a randomized controlled trial
results.tldr: It is suggested that digital tools can be effectively utilized for the comprehensive management of cognitive function and mental health well-being and contribute to promoting healthy aging and provide an efficient method for managing the mental and physical health of older adults.
results.abstract: It is suggested that digital tools can be effectively utilized for the comprehensive management of cognitive function and mental health well-being and contribute to promoting healthy aging and provide an efficient method for managing the mental and physical health of older adults.

results.title: Effects of digital health education intervention on stress, anxiety and depression of patients with polycystic ovarian syndrome: study protocol for a single blinded randomized controlled trial
results.tldr: Emerging evidence suggests that smartphone-based health education interventions could be helpful in alleviating the psychological burden associated with PCOS by improving awareness and self-management among patients.
results.abstract: Emerging evidence suggests that smartphone-based health education interventions could be helpful in alleviating the psychological burden associated with PCOS by improving awareness and self-management among patients.

results.title: Development and effectiveness evaluation of an interactive e-learning environment to enhance digital health literacy in cancer patients: study protocol for a randomized controlled trial
results.tldr: In an e-learning environment, patients will learn strategies to use when searching for reliable cancer-related online information and the e-learning environment could empower patients to retrieve more reliable information about their disease.
results.abstract: In an e-learning environment, patients will learn strategies to use when searching for reliable cancer-related online information and the e-learning environment could empower patients to retrieve more reliable information about their disease.

results.title: Effectiveness of Online Self-Learning Platforms for Promoting Digital Health Literacy: A Randomized Controlled Trial (RCT).
results.venue: Studies in Health Technology and Informatics
results.tldr: The results indicate that digital services should become increasingly relevant for promoting digital health literacy in the population.
results.abstract: The results indicate that digital services should become increasingly relevant for promoting digital health literacy in the population.

results.title: Evaluation of the efficacy of integrating digital therapeutics with home-based cardiac rehabilitation on cardiovascular health in high-risk post-PCI patients (DTx-HBCR): Study protocol for a randomized controlled trial
results.tldr: This DTx-integrated, multidisciplinary HBCR program as an alternative to usual care in high-risk patients following percutaneous coronary intervention has the potential to enhance cardiovascular health outcomes in this high-risk population.
results.abstract: This DTx-integrated, multidisciplinary HBCR program as an alternative to usual care in high-risk patients following percutaneous coronary intervention has the potential to enhance cardiovascular health outcomes in this high-risk population.

results.title: Digital health interventions for cervical cancer screening among hard-to-reach women in health-resource-limited areas: Protocol for a controlled trial with historical controls and two randomised intervention arms
results.tldr: If effective, this study will be among the first to evaluate a full-process digital health intervention that combines algorithm-based identification with a web-plus-WeChat platform for cervical-cancer screening in resource-limited areas of China.
results.abstract: If effective, this study will be among the first to evaluate a full-process digital health intervention that combines algorithm-based identification with a web-plus-WeChat platform for cervical-cancer screening in resource-limited areas of China.

results.title: Instant messaging-based digital health interventions for diabetes management: a domain-structured systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials
results.tldr: Instant-messaging interventions for diabetes do not yield a clearly favorable overall pooled effect, but they show credible benefits for behavioral outcomes and selected clinical endpoints, and should be interpreted cautiously.
results.abstract: Instant-messaging interventions for diabetes do not yield a clearly favorable overall pooled effect, but they show credible benefits for behavioral outcomes and selected clinical endpoints, and should be interpreted cautiously.
